message:
fix(core): skip HAVING queries in empty-set fixup and guard correlated subquery pre-eval

Two fixes found during fresh-eyes review:

1. The aggregate empty-set fixup (introduced in 895e69b1) incorrectly
   synthesized a default row when HAVING filtered out the aggregate
   result. In SQLite, SELECT ... HAVING <false> must return 0 rows,
   not a row with defaults. Skip the fixup when a HAVING clause is
   present — the empty result is intentional.

   Fixes: test_conformance_having_no_group_by,
          test_conformance_having_without_group_by_s231,
          test_conformance_having_without_group_by_s272

2. The DELETE scalar subquery pre-evaluation (introduced in ccda164c)
   did not check is_correlated_subquery before inlining. A correlated
   subquery like (SELECT AVG(val) FROM t AS t2 WHERE t2.cat = t.cat)
   was incorrectly pre-evaluated without the outer row context, producing
   a wrong constant and breaking the DELETE WHERE condition.

   Now both the detection function and the inlining function skip
   correlated subqueries, leaving them for per-row VDBE evaluation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
